title: Cato Threat Prevention Events (Cato Networks)
spl:
index=sase sourcetype IN ("cato:events","cato:sase") earliest=-24h
| eval blocked=if(match(lower(coalesce(action, disposition, verdict, "")),"(?i)block|prevent|deny|drop"),1,0)
| eval threat=coalesce(threat_name, signature_name, rule_name, description, "unknown")
| eval dst_service=coalesce(dst_port, service_name, app_name)
| where blocked=1 AND match(lower(coalesce(event_sub_type, category, "")),"(?i)ips|intrusion|threat|exploit")
| stats count values(threat) as threats values(dst_ip) as dst_ips by src_ip, site_name, dst_service
| sort -count
description: Cato IPS blends machine learning and signatures inline on all traversing traffic. Because enforcement runs at Cato PoPs, IPS coverage is uniform for every site and remote user without shipping appliances to each location. Tracking blocked threats, source context, targeted services, and attack patterns supports incident triage, threat hunting, and validation that prevention is active everywhere.
value: Security operations teams analyze Cato threat prevention events (IPS, Anti-Malware, DNS Security) to detect active attack campaigns, identify compromised internal hosts, and correlate threats with CVE data for targeted patching prioritization.
implementation: Enrich `src_ip` with asset and identity lookups. Create notables for rare threats, cross-site recurrence of the same source, or blocks against critical server subnets. Tune out known vulnerability scanners only with documented exceptions. Correlate spikes with Cato configuration changes (UC-17.3.28).
visualization: Table (top blocked flows), Bar chart (threats by site), Map (src_geo if present), Timeline (block burst detection).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cato Networks threat prevention events in `index=cato` with `sourcetype=cato:events` where `event_sub_type` is IPS or Anti-Malware. Key fields: `threat_name`, `threat_type` (exploit/malware/c2/botnet), `severity`, `signature_id`, `src_ip`, `dst_ip`, `dst_port`, `user_name`, `action`, `cve_id`.
• Cato threat prevention combines IPS (signature-based + anomaly), Anti-Malware (inline file scanning with ML), and DNS Security. All events share the same schema. This UC focuses on threat intelligence correlation and campaign detection.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify threat events:
```spl
index=cato sourcetype="cato:events" event_sub_type IN ("IPS", "Anti-Malware", "DNS Security") earliest=-7d
| stats count by event_sub_type, severity
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Threat campaign detection:
```spl
index=cato sourcetype="cato:events" event_sub_type IN ("IPS", "Anti-Malware") severity IN ("High","Critical") earliest=-24h
| stats count dc(src_ip) as sources dc(dst_ip) as targets values(cve_id) as cves earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen by threat_name, threat_type
| eval campaign_duration_hrs=round((last_seen - first_seen)/3600, 1)
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL: Groups high-severity threats by name and type to detect campaigns. A threat hitting many sources from many targets over hours indicates an active attack campaign. The CVE mapping helps prioritize patching. A single threat from one source to one target is likely a targeted attack. Multiple threats from one source suggests a compromised attacker host or automated attack toolkit.

Internal threat sources:
```spl
index=cato sourcetype="cato:events" event_sub_type IN ("IPS", "Anti-Malware") earliest=-24h
| where match(src_ip, "^(10\\.|172\\.(1[6-9]|2[0-9]|3[0-1])\\.|192\\.168\\.)")
| stats count dc(dst_ip) as targets by src_ip, user_name, threat_name
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In Cato Management: Monitoring > Events > filter by IPS/Anti-Malware.
(b) Download an EICAR test file and verify Anti-Malware detection.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Threat type distribution. Row 2 — Active campaigns. Row 3 — Internal threat sources.
Alert: Immediate on internal source triggering IPS/Anti-Malware events.

Runbook:
1. **Internal host triggering threats** — Likely compromised. Isolate and investigate.
2. **Campaign spanning hours** — Active external attack. Block source ranges.
3. **CVE with available patch** — Coordinate patching with affected teams.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No IPS events** — IPS may be in monitor-only mode. Check Cato Security Policy > Threat Prevention.
• **`cve_id` empty** — Not all signatures map to CVEs. Use the Cato threat library for context.
grandmaExplanation: Cato's built-in security catches malware, exploits, and suspicious connections. We group these detections to find active attack campaigns — when the same attack hits us from multiple directions, we know it is targeted and respond faster.
hardware: Cato Socket, Cato vSocket, Cato SDP Client — threats are seen and acted on at the PoP; edge devices tunnel traffic into that inspection path.
